zh.lifeSummary: 當William Payne III於1557年12月31日出生在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆時，他的父親William Payne II25歲，母親Joan22歲。他和Agnes Neves至少有4個兒子和11個女兒。他去世於1621年6月在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Payne III于1557年12月31日出生在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆时，他的父亲William Payne II25岁，母亲Joan22岁。他和Agnes Neves至少有4个儿子和11个女儿。他去世于1621年6月在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Payne III was born on 31 December 1557, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, his father, William Payne II, was 25 and his mother, Joan, was 22. He had at least 4 sons and 11 daughters with Agnes Neves. He died in June 1621, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Lavenham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Payne III est né le 31 décembre 1557, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Payne II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 11 filles avec Agnes Neves. Il est décédé en juin 1621, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Payne III am 31. Dezember 1557 in Lavenham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Payne II 25 und seine Mutter Joan 22. Er hatte mit Agnes Neves mindestens 4 Söhne und 11 Töchter. Er starb im Juni 1621 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Lavenham, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Payne III è nato il il 31 dicembre 1557 nel Lavenham, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, William Payne II, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Joan, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 11 figlie con Agnes Neves. Morì nel giugno 1621, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Lavenham, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Payne IIIが1557年12月31日にイギリススフォルクラベンハムで生まれた時，父William Payne IIは25歳，母Joanは22歳であった。彼はAgnes Nevesとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と11人の娘をもうけた。彼は1621年6月に故郷で63歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクラベンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Payne III(이)가 1557년 12월 31일 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Payne II(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Agnes Neves(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1621년 6월 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Payne III nasceu em 31 de dezembro de 1557, em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Payne II, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 11 filhas com Agnes Neves. Ele faleceu em junho de 1621, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Payne III родился 31 декабря 1557 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Payne II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan, 22. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 11 дочерей от Agnes Neves. Он умер июнь 1621 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Payne III nació el 31 de diciembre de 1557, en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Payne II, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 11 hijas con Agnes Neves. Murió en junio de 1621, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Lavenham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
